feat: Webflow-Style DOM Navigator — full recursive tree with bi-directional sync

1. Tree Engine (navigator.js):
- Recursive DOM scanner builds nested tree from canvas sections
- Icon mapping for 40+ HTML tags (H1-H6, div, a, img, button, form, etc.)
- Smart label generation: data-pc-id path → class name → text preview
- Path-based stable IDs for all elements (_pathId)
- Section groups with collapsible headers + element count badges

2. Bi-Directional Sync:
- Canvas → Navigator: click [data-pc-id] element → auto-expand tree, highlight + scroll to node
- Navigator → Canvas: click tree node → Inspector.select() + PropertyBridge.raycast() + scroll to element
- Hover Navigator item → blue outline on canvas element (live CSS injection)

3. UI/UX (navigator.css):
- Dark Pro theme matching inspector panel
- Nested list with vertical indent guide lines (::before pseudo-element)
- Collapsible sections with animated ▶ carets
- Hover: bg highlight + tag label appears
- Active node: blue tinted bg + border
- Sticky section headers while scrolling

4. Drag & Drop (native HTML5):
- All nodes are draggable
- Drop-over indicator: dashed blue border
- Sibling-only reorder → instant DOM move via insertBefore()
- Toast warning if cross-parent drop attempted

5. Integration:
- New 🌳 tab in sidebar alongside Add/Layers/Pages/💡
- switchSTab('navigator') calls Navigator.refresh()
- renderAll() auto-refreshes tree when navigator tab is active
- Navigator.init() attaches canvas click listener on DOMContentLoaded

commit.message:
feat(session18): telegram bot — real-time alerts for arb spreads and whale activity

Infrastructure:
- lib/telegram.ts: HTTP wrapper for Telegram Bot API (no SDK)
  sendMessage, setWebhook, setBotCommands, escapeMarkdown, isTelegramConfigured()
- lib/telegram-dispatcher.ts: alert dispatch engine
  threshold checks, category filters, daily rate limiting, delivery logging
  auto-deactivates subscribers who block the bot

API Routes:
- app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts: receives Telegram updates
  secret-token verification, handles /start (with link token), /status,
  /pause [1h|1d|forever], /resume, /settings, /help
- app/api/telegram/link/route.ts: generates 15-min one-time link tokens
  auth-gated (Bearer token), upserts telegram_subscribers row

Database:
- scripts/migrations/session18-telegram.sql: two new tables
  telegram_subscribers (preferences, thresholds, rate-limit counters)
  telegram_alerts_sent (delivery log per subscriber per alert)
  RLS: service role full access; users can read/update own row

Ingestion hook:
- scripts/ingest.ts: after disagreement upsert, dispatches Telegram alerts
  for all spreads >= 10pt found in the current run (top 10 by spread)

Settings UI:
- app/settings/telegram/page.tsx: full connect + preferences flow
  generate link → open Telegram → start bot → auto-link; threshold sliders;
  alert type toggles; reconnect flow; refresh status button
- app/settings/page.tsx: Telegram card linking to /settings/telegram

Docs + config:
- docs/telegram-bot-setup.md: step-by-step BotFather setup guide
- docs/env-vars.md: Telegram section (3 new variables)
- .env.local + .env.example: TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN (placeholder),
  TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET (pre-generated), TELEGRAM_BOT_USERNAME
- scripts/migrations/README.md: entry #24

Wave 6 wet-test fixes: persist state_returns + friendly CLI errors

Hard wet test exposed two defects the review missed:

1. **state_returns data loss**: pipeline state dispatch was computing
   real state returns (CA $3,661 + NY $752 on a multi-state W-2
   fixture) but only storing them on PipelineResult. The
   result.json on disk serializes through canonical.model_dump, so
   the persisted list was always empty. Fixed by copying state_returns
   onto canonical via model_copy(update=...) after dispatch.

2. **raw-traceback CLI errors**: passing a missing input directory
   or a malformed taxpayer JSON leaked the Python traceback instead
   of a friendly one-line error. CLI now catches FileNotFoundError,
   json.JSONDecodeError, and pydantic.ValidationError and prints a
   readable message with exit code 2.

New tests:
- TestCLIFriendlyErrors x 3 (missing dir, malformed JSON, schema
  mismatch — each asserts no "Traceback" in stderr and rc == 2)
- TestStateReturnsPersistedToResultJson (multi-state W-2 round-trip
  via result.json, pins the CA state return persistence)
- test_run_missing_input_dir_raises renamed to
  test_run_missing_input_dir_returns_exit_code_2 to match the new
  friendly-error semantics

Suite 3596 → 3600 (+4 new tests, zero regressions).

Add gradient blend space interpolation (HSV, HSV Far, Oklab)

Gradient stops can now blend in 4 color spaces: RGB, HSV (shortest
hue path), HSV Far (longest path), and Oklab (perceptual, default).
Oklab uses Oklch polar interpolation to preserve chroma through
transitions — eliminates muddy grey midpoints between saturated colors.

UI: clickable blend mode label on left side of gradient editor header,
plus context menu section. CSS preview approximates non-RGB blends
via 12-sample interpolation. New knot colors sample in active blend space.

Faster silent-degradation detection + wake-time health check

Two improvements informed by last night's vpn11.log (home WiFi,
VK returning 400 Bad Request ~1 permission refresh per 2-minute
cycle while phone was asleep):

1) Lower watchdog condition 3 threshold from 10 to 5 errors.

   Last night it took 16+ minutes (19 errors accumulated across 8+
   refresh cycles) before the old threshold fired, leaving the tunnel
   broken much longer than necessary. At 5 errors the same scenario
   fires at ~8 minutes, still conservative enough to ignore a single
   transient cycle.

   False-positive risk stays low because the whitelist introduced in
   build 16 only counts pion Warnf/Errorf containing "error response",
   which is the exact substring pion produces for real STUN error
   responses from the server. Network-layer drops and teardown noise
   are still excluded.

2) Wire up a wake-time fast-path health check.

   When iOS calls sleep()/wake() on the PacketTunnelProvider, the
   degradation counter may have accumulated a few pion errors during
   sleep that aren't yet enough to trip the 30-second watchdog's
   condition 3. But the moment the user unlocks the phone is exactly
   when a broken tunnel is most visible to them.

   Swift wake() now calls wgWakeHealthCheck -> Proxy.WakeHealthCheck,
   a much tighter variant: if any 2+ pion permission/binding errors
   have accumulated since the last ForceReconnect, immediately tear
   down and rebuild. The user trades ~5 seconds of reconnect latency
   (which is invisible during the wake animation) for not hitting
   a silently dead tunnel when they tap Safari.

   This complements, not replaces, the normal watchdog: the watchdog
   is tuned for slow-moving degradation over minutes, wake() catches
   the case where the user is about to look at the screen right now.

Also bumps build number 16 -> 17.

wave6-real-acroform-widgets: research and map IRS widget names for Tier-1 ingesters

Fetches the IRS fillable templates for W-2, 1099-INT, 1099-DIV, 1099-NEC,
1099-R, 1099-G, 1065 K-1, and 1120-S K-1 from https://www.irs.gov/pub/irs-pdf,
archives SHA-256-pinned copies under skill/reference/irs_forms/, and enumerates
the real AcroForm widget path for every box modeled on skill.scripts.models.
Every Tier-1 ingester now carries a unified FIELD_MAP holding both the wave-1
synthetic fixture keys and the real per-copy IRS widget names, so the same
ingester instance reads synthetic test PDFs AND real IRS fillable templates.

Per form:

- W-2 (fw2.pdf, 407 widgets): 108 real widget names across 6 copies
  (CopyA / Copy1 / CopyB / CopyC / Copy2 / CopyD), expanded from 18 templates.
  Covers employer_name/ein, boxes 1-8, 10, 11, and two state rows
  (box 15/16/17). Box 12 codes, 13 checkboxes, 14 other, and boxes 18-20
  locality are not mapped because the canonical model treats them as
  structured lists or leaves them out entirely.

- 1099-INT (f1099int.pdf, 200 widgets): 44 real widget names across 4 copies
  enumerated explicitly because per-copy container naming is inconsistent
  (LeftColumn vs LftColumn; RghtColumn vs RghtCol). Covers payer identity
  and every monetary box on Form1099INT.

- 1099-DIV (f1099div.pdf, 205 widgets): 56 real widget names across 4 copies.
  Note the Rev. 1-2022 box renumbering: real box 12 (exempt-interest dividends)
  maps to canonical box11_exempt_interest_dividends per the model's pre-2022
  numbering. Box 13 (specified PAB interest dividends) left unmapped pending
  wet-test confirmation.

- 1099-NEC (f1099nec.pdf, 113 widgets): 16 real widget names across 4 copies
  for payer identity + box 1 and box 4. Boxes 2/3/5/6/7 not mapped because
  they are not yet on Form1099NEC.

- 1099-R (f1099r.pdf, 254 widgets): 50 real widget names across 5 copies
  (CopyA uses Box10_ReadOrder while other copies use Box10; CopyA also nests
  payer TIN under a PayersTIN[0] subcontainer). Covers payer identity, box 1,
  2a, 4, 7 codes, 9a %, and the first state-row box 14/15/16.

- 1099-G (f1099g.pdf, 141 widgets): 36 real widget names across 4 copies for
  payer identity and boxes 1-7. Updated test_field_map_covers_required_boxes
  to use issubset() instead of set equality, matching the other 1099 ingester
  tests, so adding real-widget keys alongside synthetic keys does not break
  the assertion.

- Schedule K-1 1065 / 1120-S (f1065sk1.pdf / f1120ssk.pdf): 4 real widget
  names for Part I entity identity (source_ein / source_name) on both K-1
  flavors. Part III line amounts are left to fall through to _acroform_raw.*
  because each K-1 line has a (code, amount) pair and many lines repeat the
  pair for multiple entries -- decoding the exact line -> widget mapping
  needs wet-test confirmation against a filled K-1 sample, which is out of
  scope for this research pass.

- 1099-B: the IRS does NOT publish a fillable 1099-B (probed URLs returned
  HTTP 404). The IRS form the broker receives is paper-scannable / flattened;
  brokers file electronically via IRS FIRE/IRIS (XML), not AcroForms; recipient
  copies are flattened consolidated broker statements. The 1099-B Azure
  Document Intelligence ingester handles real broker summaries via the
  Unified US Tax prebuilt model -- this AcroForm ingester remains synthetic-
  only. Module docstring now documents the upgrade path.

- SSA-1099: neither the IRS nor the SSA publishes a fillable SSA-1099. The
  SSA generates it per-beneficiary and delivers via paper or the my Social
  Security portal as a flattened PDF. This AcroForm ingester cannot be
  upgraded; the upgrade path is the SSA-1099 OCR / Azure Document Intelligence
  path (reads flattened SSA-1099 PDFs directly). Module docstring updated.

Test coverage:

- Every existing synthetic-fixture test still passes unchanged (the real
  widget names are additions, not replacements, and test_field_map_... uses
  issubset so the merged map is a superset of the required synthetic keys).
- 29 new tests: for each of W-2, 1099-INT, 1099-DIV, 1099-NEC, 1099-G,
  1099-R, and Schedule K-1 (1065 + 1120-S), a TestReal<FORM>AcroForm class
  (a) asserts the archived reference PDF exists, (b) asserts the ingester
  can_handle the real PDF, (c) asserts ingest() succeeds with the correct
  document_kind (the blank IRS templates have no values set, so the test
  just confirms no crash and correct classification), and (d) asserts that
  every 'topmostSubform[0]' widget key in the FIELD_MAP resolves to an
  actual widget on the archived PDF.
- pytest skill/tests/ -x -q: 3339 passed, 3 skipped (was 3310 pre-wave6).

commit.message:
wave6-form-6251: AMT compute and render

Add a conditional Form 6251 (Alternative Minimum Tax) compute-and-render
path so returns with SALT add-backs, ISO exercises, private-activity
bond interest, or depreciation timing differences get their AMT
computed and folded into total_tax.

The engine gates the AMT pass on a cheap `_any_amt_trigger` check
(SALT > 0 on Schedule A, any AMTAdjustments entry, or 1099-INT box 9).
When fired, `compute_form_6251_fields` maps the canonical return onto
the full Form 6251 Part I + Part II line layout, applies the TY2025
exemption table ($88,100 / $137,000 / $68,500) with 25¢/$1 phase-out
starting at $626,350 / $1,252,700 / $626,350, and computes tentative
AMT using the 26%/28% schedule breakpoint at $239,100 ($119,550 MFS)
with the $4,782 ($2,391 MFS) flat adjustment. Final AMT = max(0,
TMT - regular tax) flows into `computed.alternative_minimum_tax` and
is added to `total_tax`. Part III (lines 12-40, capital-gain maximum
rates) is intentionally left at zero for wave 6 — the common SALT-
add-back case does not need it and the instructions path to Part III
requires a Qualified Dividends and Capital Gain Tax Worksheet that
the skill does not yet build.

Layer 2 overlays the IRS-hosted TY2025 Form 6251 (SHA-256 pinned,
bundled at skill/reference/irs_forms/f6251.pdf) via the wave-6 widget
map at skill/reference/form-6251-acroform-map.json. The map enumerates
all 62 widgets (33 on page 1 for Part I/II, 29 on page 2 for Part III)
in visual reading order and ties every Form6251Fields dataclass
attribute to its widget. The pipeline renders form_6251.pdf only when
`computed.alternative_minimum_tax > 0`.

Adds:
* skill/scripts/output/form_6251.py — Layer 1 compute + Layer 2 render
* skill/reference/form-6251-acroform-map.json — 62-widget map
* skill/reference/irs_forms/f6251.pdf — TY2025 source (pinned SHA)
* skill/tests/test_form_6251_compute.py — 38 tests
* skill/tests/test_form_6251_render.py — 10 tests
* AMTAdjustments model + ComputedTotals.alternative_minimum_tax field
* _any_amt_trigger gate + compute() integration
* run_pipeline render_form_6251 arg
* amt_6251 block in ty2025-constants.json (replaces _todo entry)
* 2 cross-check tests in test_ty2025_constants.py

Authority: IRS Form 6251 (TY2025), https://www.irs.gov/pub/irs-pdf/f6251.pdf
IRS Instructions for Form 6251, https://www.irs.gov/pub/irs-pdf/i6251.pdf

renderer. Adds the per-business depreciation pipeline that Schedule C
filers need to claim §179, bonus depreciation, MACRS, §280F-capped
auto depreciation, or §197 amortization.

  business_use_pct, macrs_class, section_179_elected,
  bonus_depreciation_elected, prior_year_depreciation,
  is_listed_property, is_suv_over_6000lb.

  per-business section_179_carryover_from_prior_year.

MACRS tables (skill/scripts/calc/macrs_tables.py)
-------------------------------------------------
- Half-year convention for 3/5/7/10/15/20-year GDS (Pub 946 Table A-1,
  200% DB / 150% DB baked in).
- Mid-month convention for 27.5-year residential rental (Table A-6)
  and 39-year nonresidential real property (Table A-7a), month-1 column.
- Source: IRS Publication 946 (rev. 2024), Appendix A, pinned in the
  module docstring.

Form 4562 compute (skill/scripts/output/form_4562.py)
-----------------------------------------------------
- TY2025 constants: §179 limit $1,250,000, phase-out threshold
  $3,130,000, SUV sub-cap $31,300, bonus depreciation 40% (§168(k)
  phase-down), §280F auto caps ($20,400 / $19,800 / $11,900 / $7,160).

  with Part I phase-out, §179 business-income limitation, bonus
  depreciation, MACRS, §280F auto cap, Part V listed property, and
  Part VI amortization.

  variant used by the engine's schedule_c_net_profit hook to avoid
  circular imports.

- Wave-6 widget map covering Part I lines 1-13, Part II 14-16,
  Part III line 17, Part IV line 22/23 total, Part VI line 44.
  Per-asset repeating rows (Part III Section B, Part V, Part VI row
  detail) render via attached statements in a later wave — the
  Layer 1 dataclass already exposes per-asset detail.
- render_form_4562_pdfs_all produces one filled PDF per ScheduleC
  that has depreciable_assets.

Schedule C engine hook
----------------------

commit.message:
wave6-form-8829: home office compute and render (simplified + regular)

Adds Form 8829 Expenses for Business Use of Your Home for Schedule C
filers. Two methods supported:

* Simplified: $5/sq ft capped at 300 sq ft / $1,500, with an
  additional gross-income cap so the deduction can't widen a Sch C
  loss. No Form 8829 is filed.
* Regular: Form 8829 with direct/indirect split, business-use
  percentage, operating-expense carryover (Part IV line 43), and the
  Part III 39-year straight-line mid-month MACRS depreciation
  mini-worksheet for the business portion of an owned home.

Three public layers mirror the wave-5 scaffold:

* Layer 0 compute_home_office_deduction (dispatcher) — returns the
  Sch C line 30 amount; used by the calc engine's pre-tenforty hook.
* Layer 1 compute_form_8829_fields — frozen Form8829Fields dataclass
  with one attribute per line.
* Layer 2 render_form_8829_pdf(s) — AcroForm overlay on the 2025 IRS
  f8829.pdf (SHA-pinned at 994834c9...; widget map covers all 58
  text widgets).

Integration points:

* models.HomeOffice added as an optional ScheduleC.home_office block;
  legacy callers who set line30_home_office_expense directly are
  untouched.
* calc.engine.compute calls apply_home_office_deductions first so
  downstream SE tax, AGI, and CTC see the correct Sch C net profit.
* pipeline.run_pipeline gains render_form_8829 gate; one PDF per
  regular-method Schedule C.
* paper_bundle _FORM_ORDER appends form_8829 (attachment sequence
  176, after the numbered schedules).
* schemas/return.schema.json regenerated.

Tests: 32 new (test_form_8829_compute.py 24, test_form_8829_render.py
8) covering the simplified/regular dispatcher, gross-income cap,
prior-year carryovers, mid-month first-year depreciation (January and
June), multi-business dispatch, widget-map SHA, and end-to-end PDF
round-trip. Total suite: 3342 passed / 3 skipped.

commit.message:
wave6-schedule-d-8949: compute+render for capital gains chain

Add the Schedule D (Form 1040) and Form 8949 compute + render chain
so the pipeline can produce a complete capital-gains return for any
canonical return carrying 1099-B transactions or 1099-DIV box 2a
distributions.

- skill/scripts/output/form_8949.py: Layer 1 compute_form_8949_fields
  classifies every 1099-B transaction into Part I (short-term) box
  A/B/C or Part II (long-term) box D/E/F using is_long_term and
  basis_reported_to_irs with an explicit form_8949_box_code override;
  Layer 2 render_form_8949_pdf overlays the IRS fillable PDF, one
  output file per box code (suffixed form_8949_<box>.pdf for
  multi-box returns). 11-row page cap with overflow warning.
- skill/scripts/output/schedule_d.py: Layer 1 compute_schedule_d_fields
  aggregates the 8949 pages onto lines 1b/2/3/8b/9/10, carries 1099-DIV
  cap gain distributions onto line 13, computes net ST/LT/total, and
  caps any line-16 loss at -$3,000 (-$1,500 for MFS). Layer 2 overlays
  the IRS f1040sd.pdf with SHA-256 verification.
- skill/reference/schedule-d-acroform-map.json + form-8949-acroform-map.json:
  real IRS widget maps built from the TY2025 fillable PDFs (pinned by
  SHA-256); f1040sd.pdf and f8949.pdf cached under skill/reference/irs_forms.
- skill/scripts/models.py: extend Form1099BTransaction with an optional
  form_8949_box_code override for manually-entered transactions that
  aren't on a 1099-B at all (box C / box F). Regenerate return.schema.json.
- skill/scripts/pipeline.py: add render_schedule_d kwarg to run_pipeline;
  when schedule_d_required() is True, render schedule_d.pdf plus every
  companion Form 8949 page into output_dir.
- skill/tests/test_form_8949_render.py, test_schedule_d_render.py: 46
  new tests covering Layer 1 box classification, per-page totals, wash-
  sale adjustments, 11-row overflow, $3k and $1.5k loss caps, golden
  fixture (w2_investments_itemized), Layer 2 round-trip through the
  real IRS PDF widgets, SHA-256 mismatch raising, and an end-to-end
  pipeline integration test that drops a 1099-B payload and verifies
  both schedule_d.pdf and form_8949.pdf land in output_dir.

fix: apply PER_GALLON container multiplier in portal order and quote line creation

Root cause: two action handlers bypassed resolveProductSellPrice entirely or
called it without containerGallons, causing PER_GALLON products to bill at the
raw per-gallon rate × raw container count instead of the correct
qty × gallonsPerContainer × rawPrice.

Bug 1 — app/order-requests/actions.ts (convertOrderRequestToInvoice):
  Was: unitPrice = baseUnitPrice × (1 - discountPct%) — no contract lookup,
       no priceUnitBasis, no container conversion
  Fix: resolves SiteProductSetting.containerGallons (→ Product.packageSize
       fallback) before calling resolveProductSellPrice for each line.
       Stores priceSource, baselineUnitPrice, priceUnitBasis, contractUnitPrice
       on each invoice line for full audit trail.

Bug 2 — app/quotes/actions.ts (addQuoteLine):
  Was: resolveProductSellPrice called without containerGallons, so applyBasis
       used the 1-gallon fallback — effective price = rawPrice × 1 for PER_GALLON
  Fix: fetches containerGallons from SiteProductSetting (→ Product.packageSize)
       before calling resolveProductSellPrice, same pattern as addInvoiceLine.

Confirmed fix (6 × Bug and Wheel Cleaner, 55 gal, $18.90/gal):
  Before: 6 × $18.90 = $113.40   (wrong)
  After:  6 × (55 × $18.90) = 6 × $1,039.50 = $6,237.00   (correct)

lib/sellPrice.ts: export applyBasis for direct unit testing.
lib/sellPrice.test.ts: 11 new tests covering PER_GALLON, PER_CONTAINER,
  site-specific override, and edge cases (null/0 gallons fallback).

fix: users JOIN が null になりページが crash する問題を service_role で修正

users テーブルの RLS は users_select_own (auth.uid() = id) しか
無いため、team_members や attendances から users を JOIN しても
他メンバーの users 行が NULL で返ってくる。
クライアント側 (例: MemberList が member.users.name.charAt(0)) で
NULL アクセスして React がクラッシュ → 「This page couldn't load」
というエラーになっていた。

対象ページを service_role 経由に切り替えて RLS をバイパス。
各ページは layout 側で認可済みなので情報漏洩リスクは無し。

- members/page.tsx: ホスト権限。team_members + users

- ng-list/page.tsx: ホスト権限。NGペア + 全メンバー
- attendance/page.tsx: ホスト権限。出席統計用のメンバー一覧

  (これは host/guest 両方が触るので影響範囲が広い)
